analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$77,558 as of Apr 26 08:10 UTC (+0.33% in 24h). Range $77,500-$78,000. Key resistance $80,000 unconfirmed. Support $77,000 / $75,831.
sentiment_key_signals: Fear & Greed Index: at 3-month high (highest since mid-January 2026) — driven by short squeeze + ETF inflows
Short squeeze supporting price but trend unconfirmed per investing.com — RSI neutral zone
Goldman Sachs filed SEC registration for new Bitcoin ETF (after acquiring Innovator Capital Management Apr 2)
Morgan Stanley also filed for spot BTC ETF — major bank institutional embrace ongoing
Strategy bought 34,164 BTC for $2.54B (Apr week) — largest institutional buy since 2024
KelpDAO $292M DeFi exploit caused DeFi TVL -$14B; altcoin sentiment fragile; BTC isolated
ETF inflows steady; capital concentrating in BTC large-cap vs altcoin lag
FOMC Apr 28-29 (2 days): Warsh hawkish tone = rate cut expectations fading = mild headwind
Iran ceasefire extended by Trump — geopolitical tail risk temporarily reduced
Volume below average — price action lacking full conviction; pre-FOMC vol suppression
analysis.macro_backdrop: No CPI/PPI/NFP in next 24h. FOMC Apr 28-29 is dominant near-term risk (2 days away) — rate hold at 3.50-3.75% expected but hawkish Warsh rhetoric risk. GDP Q1 advance + Core PCE due Apr 30 (4 days). Iran ceasefire extended — geopolitical risk reduced. Goldman Sachs + Morgan Stanley BTC ETF filings add bullish structural backdrop.
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral (0.47): BTC consolidating $77K-$78K with mixed signals. Bullish: 3-month high sentiment index, steady ETF inflows, institutional accumulation (Strategy $2.54B), Goldman/Morgan Stanley ETF filings, Iran ceasefire. Bearish: Short squeeze (not organic buying), trend unconfirmed at $80K resistance, FOMC countdown with hawkish Warsh risk, altcoin sentiment fragile, below-avg volume. Confidence 0.47 < 0.50 threshold — macro filter INACTIVE this cycle. Regime=neutral.
